name: Valve Seat LeakTest Procedure
expected_response_detailed: API 598 low pressure seat test: 80 psig air or gas test at both directions. Acceptable leakage: 0 bubbles for resilient seats (soft), 0 bubbles metal-to-metal. High pressure: 110% of rated pressure, duration 1× diameter minutes. Procedure: isolate valve, pressurize body cavity, check downstream for no flow. Bubble test: bubble counter in water. Alternative: pressure hold decay method. Soft seats: zero leakage acceptable. Metal seats: API allows minimal leakage if specified rate acceptable.

name: Pipeline Right-of-Way Patrol
expected_response_detailed: Aerial patrol: 26 CFR 192.705 every 2 weeks in Class 3/4, monthly Class 1/2. Ground patrol: weekly in populated areas. Elements: vegetation clearing, excavation activity, erosion, exposed pipe, third-party activity, encroachments, access road condition, marker visibility. Documentation: GPS coordinate, photo, immediate hazards reported, 911 if emergency. Aircraft: visual above ground, 100-300 feet altitude, weather permitting. Helicopter: may hover for closer look.

name: Compressor Valve Inspection
expected_response_detailed: Reciprocating compressor valves: poppet or plate type. Inspection intervals: run hours based on service (2,000-8,000 hours typical). Items: seat condition, spring strength (check vs new), wear on actuators, lifting mechanism, crack detection, carbon buildup, seat flatness. Testing: bench test before installation, leak test at 50 psig air. Reassembly: proper torque, orientation. Performance: reduced capacity, increased discharge temp indicate valve issues.

name: Underwater Pipeline Survey
expected_response_detailed: Subsea inspection: ROV survey for exposed pipe, span measurement, anode depletion (electrical potential), macrofouling, coating damage. Span correction: grout bags, mechanical supports. Depth of burial verification. Cathodic protection: contact cell for potential measurement. Frequency: annual if exposed, less if buried. Water depth: diver inspection if shallow, ROV if deep. Acoustic: depth of cover sonar. Pipeline location: accurate to ±1 meter. Sediment changes: erosion or accumulation.

name: Tank Floor Thickness Survey
expected_response_detailed: API 653 tank floor: UT grid pattern typically 10-foot grid, additional points under shell. Minimum 5 readings per plate. Remaining life calculation: thickness data, corrosion rate, pit depth. Acceptable: above minimum structural per API 653 Table 4.2. Repair: patch plates if localized, complete floor replacement if widespread. Hydrostatic test after repairs. Product tightness: bottom tested if internal lining required. Shell plates also surveyed if suspected thinning.

name: Welder Qualification Records
expected_response_detailed: API 1104 welder qualification: valid 6 months minimum, must have continuity (weld within 6 months). Essential variables: position, progression, pipe diameter limits, wall thickness range, process (SMAW, GTAW, FCAW), filler metal, current/polarity. PQ test: guided bend (root and face), nick-break (fillet). Limits: diameter range qualified ±2× test diameter. Renewals: requalify if continuity lost. Record retention: 6 years minimum as-built. Visual acceptance: accept only undercuts, porosity per limits.

name: Pipeline Composite Repair
expected_response_detailed: ASME PCC-2 Article 4.1 composite sleeve: external corrosion repair, dents with <6% metal loss, gouges. Design: 4× defect length minimum sleeve, prep to near-white metal. Application: epoxy filler for profile, wrap with tension, overlap per procedure. Curing time temperature dependent. Qualification: 2-year testing per ISO TS 24817. Limitations: not for circumferential defects, not for cracks, not for internal corrosion. Inspection: holiday post-repair.

name: Regulator Inspection Frequency
expected_response_detailed: 192.740 pressure regulator inspection: each calendar year but not exceeding 15 months. Visual: external condition, setpoint pressure, response time, filter condition, relief valve operation. Testing: accuracy ±2% typical, inlet screen flow capacity. Slam-shut: trip on high/low test. Relief valve: lift setting verification, reseat tightness. Documentation: setpoint pressure, test results, maintenance performed. Replacement: if inaccurate or worn.

name: Girth Weld Rejection Criteria
expected_response_detailed: API 1104 automatic reject: any crack, any incomplete penetration, incomplete fusion >2 inches cumulative, undercut depth >1/32 inch or 12.5% wall, porosity exceeding limits, slag inclusions >2 inches cumulative. Linear indications: length, depth criteria by type. Repair: if repair requires then cut-out. Cut-out: remove defect plus 2 inches minimum each side, new weld full circumference. Documentation: NDE report showing defect location, size, type, disposition.

name: Pipeline Test Lead Examination
expected_response_detailed: Test lead stations: wire bond to pipe, box for access, typically at mile markers, cased crossings, isolating joints, rectifier locations. Inspection: bond integrity, cable condition, box lid secure, accessibility clearing. Function: CP potential measurement, cathodic interference testing, coupon monitoring. Frequency: annual with CP survey. Repair: re-bond if resistance high, cable replacement if damaged. Documentation: station number, GPS, reading values.
